content: cross-rs: zero-setup Docker-based cross compilation, supports aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu out of the box, runs QEMU for testing, slowest but most compatible. cargo-zigbuild: uses zig as linker, lighter than cross, best for musl targets and glibc version pinning, simpler setup. Native runners: macOS can compile aarch64-apple-darwin natively on macos-latest (Apple Silicon available). For claudebar (no C deps beyond system libc): cross-rs is the least-friction choice for aarch64-linux; macos native runners handle both darwin targets.
